# Repository secrets the public cinatra-ai/cinatra repo needs.
#
# A no-history snapshot drops repo settings, so these must be (re)provisioned on
# the public repo at launch. Values are NEVER committed here — only the names,
# scopes, and provisioning notes. (GitHub Actions secrets are write-only; they
# cannot be read back, so nothing here can leak a value.)

CINATRA_RELEASE_DISPATCH_TOKEN
  Purpose : cross-repo repository_dispatch from build-image.yml to
            cinatra-ai/ops (event `cinatra-release-published`) on a v* tag, so
            ops deploys the freshly-built GHCR digest.
  Type    : fine-grained PAT (or GitHub App installation token).
  Scope   : resource owner cinatra-ai, repository cinatra-ai/ops, Contents: write
            (ONLY — nothing else).
  Naming  : MUST be distinct from CINATRA_OPS_DISPATCH_TOKEN, which is the
            cinatra-ai/docs -> ops docs-dispatch PAT (see ops
            scripts/rotation-matrix.json). A separate PAT covers the marketplace
            dispatch. Reusing one name across docs / marketplace / cinatra-app
            release sources is a rotation/revocation footgun.
  Wiring  : build-image.yml reads
            `${{ secrets.CINATRA_RELEASE_DISPATCH_TOKEN || secrets.CINATRA_OPS_DISPATCH_TOKEN }}`
            — the fallback keeps the dispatch working until this token is
            provisioned, then takes precedence. Provision it before the first
            public release tag, then drop the fallback.
  Owner   : record owner, expiry, and rotation policy in ops rotation-matrix.json.

DEV_LOCK_BUMP_TOKEN
  Purpose : the scheduled/dispatch dev-lock auto-bump workflow
            (.github/workflows/dev-lock-auto-bump.yml) pushes a rolling branch
            and opens/refreshes the bump PR. The default GITHUB_TOKEN cannot
            create pull requests under this repo's Actions policy, so the
            workflow uses this operator-provisioned least-privilege token.
  Type    : fine-grained PAT (or GitHub App installation token).
  Scope   : resource owner cinatra-ai, repository cinatra-ai/cinatra,
            Contents: write + Pull requests: write (ONLY).
  Wiring  : referenced ONLY in dev-lock-auto-bump.yml as
            `${{ secrets.DEV_LOCK_BUMP_TOKEN }}` (GH_TOKEN); never echoed.
  Owner   : record owner, expiry, and rotation policy in ops
            rotation-matrix.json.

CINATRA_OPS_DISPATCH_TOKEN
  Purpose : LEGACY fallback for the build-image.yml release dispatch to
            cinatra-ai/ops on a v* tag, used only until
            CINATRA_RELEASE_DISPATCH_TOKEN is provisioned (then dropped). Shares
            its NAME with the cinatra-ai/docs -> ops docs-dispatch PAT (see ops
            scripts/rotation-matrix.json); the two are SEPARATE secrets in
            different repos.
  Type    : fine-grained PAT (or GitHub App installation token).
  Scope   : resource owner cinatra-ai, repository cinatra-ai/ops, Contents: write
            (ONLY).
  Wiring  : build-image.yml reads it as the fallback half of
            `${{ secrets.CINATRA_RELEASE_DISPATCH_TOKEN || secrets.CINATRA_OPS_DISPATCH_TOKEN }}`.
  Owner   : record owner, expiry, and rotation policy in ops rotation-matrix.json.

CINATRA_TEST_DB_URL
  Purpose : optional CI-provided Postgres URL that ENABLES the packages/agents
            integration-test step in build-image.yml. When the secret is empty
            the step is skipped (the other integration suites use a service
            container and need no secret).
  Type    : a Postgres connection URL (postgres://… ) for a disposable CI DB.
  Scope   : CI-only, least-privilege on a throwaway test database — never a
            production DSN.
  Wiring  : build-image.yml gates `Integration tests (packages/agents)` on
            `env.CINATRA_TEST_DB_URL != ''` and passes
            `${{ secrets.CINATRA_TEST_DB_URL }}`.
  Owner   : record owner, expiry, and rotation policy in ops rotation-matrix.json.

OPENAI_API_KEY
  Purpose : enables the live golden-set calibration job in skill-match-eval.yml
            (the skills matcher prompt eval). Double-gated (key present AND
            GOLDEN_EVAL_LIVE=1); forks/PRs without the secret skip cleanly.
  Type    : an OpenAI API key.
  Scope   : least-privilege OpenAI key for the eval account; CI use only.
  Wiring  : skill-match-eval.yml reads `${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}`; never
            echoed.
  Owner   : record owner, expiry, and rotation policy in ops rotation-matrix.json.

WORKS_AFTER_OPENAI_API_KEY
  Purpose : supplies the OpenAI key to the works-after-proof.yml graphiti arm
            ONLY on a trusted workflow_dispatch run (on PRs the arm is a
            secret-free skip). Kept DISTINCT from OPENAI_API_KEY so the
            dispatch-only lane has its own rotation surface.
  Type    : an OpenAI API key.
  Scope   : least-privilege OpenAI key; reaches the harness only on the trusted
            dispatch lane.
  Wiring  : works-after-proof.yml passes
            `${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && secrets.WORKS_AFTER_OPENAI_API_KEY || '' }}`.
  Owner   : record owner, expiry, and rotation policy in ops rotation-matrix.json.

# Optional (forwarded only when set; devApp repos are currently public):
WP_DRUPAL_UAT_DEPLOY_KEY
  Purpose : OPTIONAL SSH deploy key forwarded by wp-drupal-uat.yml when present,
            so a future flip of the devApp repos (wordpress-plugin /
            drupal-module / a2a-servers-dev) to PRIVATE keeps working unchanged.
            The repos are public today, so anonymous HTTPS clone needs no key
            and the secret is unset.
  Type    : an SSH private deploy key (read access to the devApp repos).
  Scope   : read-only deploy key on the devApp repos; CI use only.
  Wiring  : wp-drupal-uat.yml forwards `${{ secrets.WP_DRUPAL_UAT_DEPLOY_KEY }}`
            as the shared action's `deploy-key:`.
  Owner   : record owner, expiry, and rotation policy in ops rotation-matrix.json.

# Optional (arms the scheduled governance-drift-gate; absent => the gate green-skips):
GOVERNANCE_DRIFT_READ_TOKEN
  Purpose : arms the scheduled governance-drift-gate.yml so it can read live
            branch protection + the tag/baseline rulesets and diff them against
            the committed .github manifests (cinatra-ai/engineering#315). While
            unset the gate green-skips with a ::notice; once provisioned it
            detects governance drift.
  Type    : fine-grained PAT (or GitHub App installation token).
  Scope   : resource owner cinatra-ai, repository cinatra-ai/cinatra
            Administration: read; plus org Administration (read) so the org
            `baseline-protection` ruleset (with bypass_actors) is visible. No
            write scope.
  Wiring  : governance-drift-gate.yml passes it as the reusable workflow's
            `governance_read_token` secret; never echoed.
  Owner   : record owner, expiry, and rotation policy in ops rotation-matrix.json.

# Optional (public-distribution mirror, Release-continuity item 6):
DOCKERHUB_USERNAME / DOCKERHUB_TOKEN
  Purpose : mirror the GHCR runtime image to docker.io/cinatra/cinatra on every
            v* tag (and on demand) so a public `docker pull` works without a GHCR
            login. Used by the decoupled mirror workflow
            .github/workflows/dockerhub-publish.yml — NOT by build-image.yml,
            which remains the sole GHCR builder/publisher.
  Type    : DOCKERHUB_USERNAME = the Docker Hub account/org login; DOCKERHUB_TOKEN
            = a Docker Hub access token (NOT the account password).
  Scope   : push to the `cinatra` Docker Hub org, push only.
  Wiring  : dockerhub-publish.yml reads ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }} /
            ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }} for the Docker Hub login; the GHCR
            source is read with the workflow GITHUB_TOKEN (packages: read).
  Status  : required for the Docker Hub mirror. Both secrets are provisioned on
            the public repo.
